text.tspan: FACT OF LIFE: most messaging is AT-LEAST-ONCE delivery
u2022 Kafka, SQS, RabbitMQ, NATS u2014 all redeliver on failure
u2022 a consumer that crashes after processing but before ack u2192 retry
u2022 a producer that retries after timeout u2192 duplicate published
u2022 a network blip during ack u2192 broker thinks failed; redelivers
u2192 DUPLICATES are NORMAL, not exceptional. Design for them.

g.text: WITH IDEMPOTENT CONSUMER u2014 the fix

text.tspan: u2022 SECOND CHARGE on the card (lawsuit-grade bug)
u2022 Same DB as business logic (recommended u2014 atomic with side-effect)
u2022 Redis SET with TTL (lightweight; suitable when business logic is external)
u2022 Per-message-ID column with UNIQUE constraint (DB enforces, simplest)
KEY CONSTRAINT: the dedup record and the side-effect MUST be ATOMIC.
Otherwise: crash between them u2192 either skipped or duplicated.
